meta.title: NOR 1–2 ENG · A Quarter-final in Data
meta.description: Norway 1-2 England, FIFA World Cup 2026 quarter-final, told minute by minute through data.

hero.subHtml: The first ever World Cup meeting between the two. The furthest Norway has ever gone. And Jude Bellingham, twice, dragging England into a semi-final in the Miami heat.

timeline.heading: How it happened
timeline.lede: Six moments decided this match. The scroll bar is your match clock.

events.title: Hot, muggy, hostile
events.textHtml: A July afternoon in South Florida, 64,000 inside Hard Rock Stadium. Norway arrive having never been this far. England arrive having survived Mexico in the round of 32, rescued that night by Bellingham.

events.title: Schjelderup stuns England
events.textHtml: Andreas Schjelderup gives Norway the lead against the run of England's early pressure. The red wall of Norwegian fans erupts. Haaland's pre-match mind games suddenly look justified.

events.title: Bellingham answers before the break
events.textHtml: On the stroke of half time, Jude Bellingham strikes. Just as he did against Mexico, England's number 10 shows up exactly when the game demands it. 1–1 at the interval, and the momentum flips.

events.title: Norway's winner that wasn't
events.textHtml: From a corner, Torbjørn Heggem bundles the ball home and Norway think they lead again. Video review rules it out: Haaland pushed an England player to the ground just before the delivery. Regulation ends 1–1.

events.title: Bellingham. Again.
events.textHtml: Deep into added time, a loose ball breaks England's way and Bellingham buries the winner, his second of the night. Norway protest furiously, and not without reason. More on that below.

events.title: England hold on through extra time
events.textHtml: Thirty more minutes in the heat, a Norwegian yellow card at 117', and England see it out. Norway's greatest ever World Cup run ends. England march on to a semi-final against Argentina.

stats.heading: The numbers behind it
stats.lede: Tighter than the scoreline suggests. Norway created plenty. England were simply more precise where it counted.
stats.note: Source: live match data feed, 11 July 2026. Figures over 120 minutes.

charts.heading: The match in charts
charts.lede: Hover or tap anything below. Every segment is the real feed data.

radar.title: Head to head · six-axis radar
radar.sideHtml: Each axis is scaled to whichever team led it, so the outer edge means best in match. Norway's shape leans toward corners and set-piece territory. England's leans toward precision and control.
radar.note: Click a team to isolate the other's shape. Hover any vertex for the raw number.

controversy.heading: Two calls Norway will replay forever

cards.title: Haaland's push
cards.textHtml: Heggem's second-half finish from a corner was ruled out on video review after Haaland shoved an England player to the ground moments before the kick was taken. From potential 2–1 lead to nothing in ninety seconds.

cards.title: Did the wire change the game?
cards.textHtml: Replays suggested the Norwegian keeper's clearance before Bellingham's winner clipped the overhead sky cam wire, changing the ball's flight before it dropped to England. FIFA said the ball's sensor data showed no evidence its trajectory was affected. Norway's bench disagreed. Loudly.
